items.title: Iran believes they hold the cards amid 'dysfunction & chaos in US national security decision-making'
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Aaron David Miller, former State Department Middle East negotiator, and Senior Fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace. Miller has spent decades working in diplomacy, and he does not see a coherent strategy playing out here, just improvisation shaped by pressure, personality, and shifting leverage. The current negotiations surrounding Iran, Israel, and Lebanon are not driven by trust or a shared vision of resolution, but by tactical necessity and asymmetric perceptions of advantage. According to Miller, the United States is seeking an exit from a conflict it chose to enter, while Iran perceives itself as strategically ascendant and therefore in no rush to compromise, despite widespread damage from relentless airstrikes. The diplomatic architecture being assembled, through intermediaries, informal envoys, and unclear mandates, reflects not strength, but fragmentation within decision-making processes. Progress will not emerge, Miller argues, from symbolic gestures or improvised channels, but from direct, disciplined negotiation grounded in an understanding of history, geography, and the legitimate interests of all actors involved.

items.title: 'Undeterred’: Hungarian investigative journalist faces threats and espionage accusations from Orbán
items.description: Gavin Lee is pleased to welcome Szabolcs Panyi, VSquare’s Budapest-based lead investigative editor in charge of Central European investigations and investigative journalist at Direkt36. His reporting into alleged ties between Hungary’s political leadership and Russian state actors triggered an unprecedented response: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán publicly accused him of espionage. According to Panyi, Orbán seeks to discredit independent journalism with false narratives of conspiracies against the government.

items.title: Live: Hungary votes in crucial election that could oust populist Orban
items.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban faces his biggest challenge on Sunday as the country votes in crucial elections that could hand victory to his centre-right rival Peter Magyar. Orban, an ally of US President Donald Trump and one of the European Union's biggest antagonists, has run an aggressive campaign, claiming that a defeat would drag his country into war alongside Ukraine. Follow our liveblog for all the latest developments.

items.title: Orban and Magyar face off in historic vote
items.description: Hungarians are heading to the polls for a pivotal election in the country. Orban, 62, who is seeking a fifth straight term, has transformed his country into a model of illiberal democracy, following Trump in casting migration and "woke" values as a "civilisational" threat. Former government insider Magyar, 45, burst onto the scene just two years ago, amassing support against the backdrop of economic stagnation, despite an electoral system skewed in favour of Orban's Fidesz party. Senior fellow for Europe at the Council on Foreing Relations, Liana Fix, shares further insights.

items.title: US and Iran end 21-hour ceasefire talks without agreement before Vance departs Pakistan
items.description: The United States and Iran ended a historic round of face-to-face talks early Sunday without reaching an agreement and the fate of the fragile, two-week ceasefire still unclear. Vice President JD Vance, who led the US delegation during the 21 hours of talks in Pakistan's capital Islamabad, said negotiations finished without a deal after the Iranians refused to accept American terms to refrain from developing a nuclear weapon. France24 correspondent Fraser Jackson shares his analysis from Washington.

items.title: Hezbollah slams planned Lebanese talks with Israel as strikes kills 10
items.description: The move "exacerbates domestic divisions at a time when Lebanon most needs solidarity and internal unity to face Israel's aggression and preserve civil peace", says Hezbollah lawmaker Hassan Fadlallah in a statement

items.title: Atlantic Music Expo: Cape Verde celebrates its musical heritage

items.title: Orban's 16-year grip on power under threat from Magyar
items.description: Hungarians began voting on Sunday in closely watched parliamentary elections that could end nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orban's 16-year stint in power as a self-described "thorn" in the EU's side. Orban, 62, who is seeking a fifth straight term, has transformed his country into a model of illiberal democracy, following Trump in casting migration and "woke" values as a "civilisational" threat. Former government insider Magyar, 45, burst onto the scene just two years ago, amassing support against the backdrop of economic stagnation, despite an electoral system skewed in favour of Orban's Fidesz party. Senior Reporter at 24.hu, Zsolt Kerner, shares his insights on this pivotal election and how the Hungarian electoral system works.

items.title: Milei meets Kast after failed arrest of Chilean ex-guerrilla
items.description: Argentina’s President hosts Chilean counterpart in Buenos Aires as ties deepen; Visit overshadowed by failed arrest of ex-guerrilla wanted by Santiago as leaders seek closer political and economic alignment. Leer más

items.title: Why Anthropic’s new AI model is too powerful to release
items.description: One of the world's leading AI companies has built a model so powerful that it refuses to fully release it publicly just yet, prompting urgent talks from Wall Street to financial regulators in the UK.

items.title: Recycling's next big thing — or big bluff?
items.description: An advanced recycling process claims it can handle hard-to-recycle plastics, like packaging. But critics say it's not worth the effort.

items.title: News24 | Nigeria: At least 20 killed in attack, residents say
items.description: Authorities in Nigeria’s restive north put the death toll at three, but residents and humanitarians point to a higher toll. The attack occurred in an area where kidnapping gangs and Islamist militants are active.

items.title: Has Coachella festival sold out?
items.description: One of the world's biggest open-air music and art festivals has been hit by controversies and declining credibility — yet the world's biggest acts continue to headline.

items.title: News24 | Automatic US draft pool registration for 18-year-olds part of Trump ‘options’ for war
items.description: Eligible US men aged 18 to 25 will now be automatically registered for the military draft pool by December as part of an effort to streamline the previous process of self-registration and save money.

items.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry
items.description: In less than 48 hours, the Orion spacecraft is due to splash down in the Pacific Ocean. Crew members have said what they saw in space will forever change how they see the world.

items.title: Argentina’s Congress to debate easing glacier protection law to boost mining
items.description: Activists and demonstrators are set to take to the streets later today in protest at debate over changes to Argentina's National Glacier Law. Leer más

items.title: Hezbollah | Ayatollah’s allies in Lebanon
items.description: After Israel killed Hassan Nasrallah in September 2024 and launched a ground offensive in Lebanon, many saw Hezbollah weakened; but over the past 40-plus days, the ‘Party of God’ has fired hundreds of rockets and drones, denying an easy victory to the invading Israeli forces

items.title: Authorities in Argentina say fatal school shooting linked online ‘TCC’ crime community
items.description: Security Minister Alejandra Monteoliva says San Cristóbal school shooting was not “an isolated case nor a case of bullying,” claiming the shooter “participated in an international digital network” and Internet subculture dedicated to such incidents. Leer más

items.title: After Iran talks falter, the big question is what happens next?
items.description: Twenty-one hours was not enough to end 47 years of hostility between Iran and the US, writes the BBC's Lyse Doucet.

items.title: Ireland clears refinery blockade as fuel protests trigger nationwide shortages
items.description: Authorities used force to reopen the country's only refinery, after days of disruption over petrol prices left fuel pumps dry and threatened emergency services operations.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Argentine author Samanta Schweblin wins one-million-euro literary prize
items.description: Argentine author takes inaugural €1m award for 'El buen mal'; Controversy surrounds state-backed Aena’s entry into the literary sphere with Premio Aena de Narrativa Hispanoamericana prize. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Israel locked into ‘forever war’ as it builds buffer zones across Middle East
items.description: Even as the US and Iran seek to cement a ceasefire, Israel is seizing more territory from its neighbours in preparation for a long, drawn-out conflict across the Middle East.

items.title: India, U.S. to deepen nuclear ties, explore LPG exports
items.description: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri meets U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright; talks focus on advancing energy security, deepening energy trade, says Indian Embassy

items.title: Report: Milei government not supporting campaign to restore dictatorship identities
items.description: Milei administration has interrupted sending of DNA kits to consulates nationwide for those who have doubts about their identity and suspect they may be children of disappeared. Leer más

items.title: Djibouti President Ismail Omar Guelleh is re-elected for sixth term
items.description: Ismail Omar Guelleh faced a single challenger, Mohamed Farah Samatar, a former ruling party member, in a race analysts say offered little genuine competition.

items.title: News24 | Sentebale launches legal action against Prince Harry over ‘reputational harm’
items.description: Sentebale, a charity co-founded by Prince Harry, is suing him for harming its reputation through a media campaign.

items.title: OpenAI identifies security issue involving third-party tool, says user data was not accessed
Poland: "Frog Patrol" saves thousands of amphibians during migration
items.description: Volunteers near Warsaw form a frog patrol each spring, helping thousands of frogs and toads cross a road safely and protect local breeding grounds.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: From chainsaw to pay rise: Argentina’s ministers cash in despite Milei austerity drive
items.description: Government decree lifts top officials’ wages to over eight million pesos a month, fuelling debate over austerity promises; Salary surge contrasts with Javier Milei’s pledge to cut political privilege. Leer más

items.title: Interstate fans say keep Gather Round in Adelaide as AFL boss probed on why AFLW not part of the program
items.description: Crows AFLW player Ebony Marinoff has questioned the AFL's CEO on why the women's program hasn't been part of the successful Gather Round in Adelaide.

items.title: News24 | Trump weighs pulling some US troops from Europe amid NATO strains, official says
items.description: US President Donald Trump, upset at NATO allies’ failure to ⁠help secure the Strait of Hormuz and angry that his plans to acquire Greenland have ⁠not moved forward, has discussed with advisersthe option of removing some US troops from Europe, a senior White House official told Reuters on Thursday.

items.title: 68 gender-based killings in Argentina in first quarter, report finds
items.description: Report by La Casa del Encuentro NGO recorded 68 gender-based killings in January-March period; 72% of killings took place in victim’s home. Leer más

items.title: Russian psyops and the dance of the candidates
items.description: The Milei administration is on the back foot, tired, hanging off the ropes, but is nowhere near knockout territory. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Republicans block bid to rein in Trump’s war powers, Democrats vow to force a vote
items.description: US House of Representatives Republicans blocked a Democratic effort to end US attacks on Iran, as Donald Trump’s party continued to prevent efforts to rein in the Republican president’s war powers.

items.title: Hungary: Orban and Magyar on the home stretch
items.description: Hungarians headed to the polls on Sunday in a pivotal parliamentary election that could end Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s 16-year grip on power, as the opposition led by Peter Magyar challenges his nationalist rule in a contest drawing scrutiny across Europe and backing for Orban from US President Donald Trump. Lecturer in comparative politics at Keele University, Gemma Loomes, shares more insights on the vote.

items.title: Legendary Bollywood singer Asha Bhosle dies aged 92
items.description: The singer recorded over 12,000 songs and was one of Indian cinema's most celebrated voices.

items.title: Calls for greater protections for Rottnest after Indigenous remains discovered
items.description: Aboriginal leaders call for greater protections for Rottnest Island after the discovery of Indigenous remains during construction work at the popular holiday resort last week.

items.title: World Bank sees Argentina’s economy growing 3.6% this year
items.description: Economy will grow 3.6% this year and 3.7% next year; Multilateral institution’s forecast means Argentina is currently on track to record three years of consecutive growth for first time since 2008. Leer más

items.title: Germany: Fare evasion is a crime that can send you to prison
items.description: In Germany, riding without a ticket on public transportation can land you in jail. The justice minister would like to change this, but her conservative coalition partners are skeptical.

items.title: NASA's billion-dollar space race goes into overdrive
items.description: NASA's renewed push to get a human mission to the moon and Mars is neither cheap, nor for the faint of heart. But who ends up covering the costs and what does everyone still on Earth get out of all this?

items.title: Israel vows to fight on as Trump grants Iran ceasefire
items.description: United States and Iran agree a ceasefire and are due to launch talks in Pakistan aimed at reaching a longer-term deal, but crucial sticking points remain. Leer más

items.title: US judge blocks Arizona criminal case against Kalshi at CFTC's request
News24 | Dutch minister says not planning to bar Kanye West
items.description: The Dutch minister says there are no plans to ban Kanye West (Ye) from the Netherlands, despite the UK banning him.

items.title: Hacking women's political underrepresentation, Dutch-style
items.description: Some 504 women were elected in recent Dutch local elections, with a "Vote for a Woman" campaign boosting their chances to gather preferential votes. Despite the record figure, women remain underrepresented.

items.title: Nigeria’s aso-oke weaving tradition gains global spotlight
items.description: In Iseyin, a quiet town in southwestern Nigeria, shaded corners and narrow alleys have become busy hubs for weaving aso-oke, a traditional Yoruba fabric. Long produced by hand, the thick, often brightly colored textile is deeply rooted in cultural heritage and identity.

items.title: Watch: BBC asks Artemis II crew a question in space
items.description: The BBC’s Science Editor Rebecca Morelle has spoken to the Artemis II crew ahead of their likely splashdown on Saturday.

items.title: Iran and Strait of Hormuz: A lone battle for control
items.description: Historically, international straits have been flashpoints between littoral states and external powers, often serving as bargaining tools during conflicts

items.title: Argentina tweaks FX rules after cost of getting dollars out surges
items.description: Milei government issues new rules for investors moving money out of the country after increased use of the parallel foreign-exchange market drove cost of trades up. Leer más

items.title: 'It's a special thing to be on Planet Earth': Artemis crew welcomed home in Houston
items.description: The four astronauts flew around the Moon in a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans in history.

items.title: UFOs: 'Most are reported by people out for an evening smoke'
items.description: Hansjürgen Köhler has been a volunteer UFO hunter for more than 50 years. He sees his work as scientific investigation, but sometimes also as caring for others.

items.title: Benin holds presidential election amid deteriorating security situation
items.description: Benin is facing harsh economic conditions and security challenges that its new leader will have to address.

items.title: Love story: 'It's not my kidney now, it's yours. I'm just holding it for you'
items.description: Despite a 10-year age gap and geographical separation, Sajarn and Codey Stow have retained a close bond since childhood. That bond has only strengthened since Codey received a donor kidney from his brother last November.

items.title: Trump orders blockade of Hormuz Strait
items.description: US navy will begin blockading ships trying to enter or leave the Strait of Hormuz “effective immediately”, US President Donald Trump said in a social media post Sunday. He added that the US would ‌also “interdict” every ‌vessel in international waters that had paid a toll to Iran for passing through the critical waterway. France 24 correspondent Fraser Jackson tells us the latest.
